summary: Cross-chapter audit of 18-chapter, 110,295-word manuscript. REDUNDANCY: 14 character re-introduction/setting re-description findings from the mandatory comparison protocol, including adjacent-chapter re-descriptions of the Mark examination (Ch2->Ch3), examination room (Ch6->Ch8), Hermione's work-mode appearance (hair, sleeves, ink across Ch2/4/6/10/12/14/15/18), Harry's honest-friend identity package (Ch5/13/17), and Malfoy grey-eyes inheritance (Ch4/18). 9 proof-of-baseline findings: Mark pain re-proved 72 times across all 18 chapters; resonance re-proved 121 times (Ch11: 27). 18 em-dash density findings: manuscript at 42.0% (threshold 10%), every chapter 30-59%. 18 She-opener findings: manuscript at 14.6% (threshold 7%), worst Ch3 at 30.8%. 13 He-opener findings: manuscript at 10.9%, worst Ch2 at 23.5%. 3 near-verbatim findings: 'channels that no longer connected' repeated across Ch2/8/12/17. 14 hedge-density findings: 'something' at 2.97/800w (410 instances), 'the way' at 1.93/800w (266 instances). 3 image-density findings: precision/precise (108), clinical (29), silence (103). CONSISTENCY: 2 CRITICAL timeline contradictions (November in December-set Ch12; January in Ch16 vs December in Ch17-18), 1 CRITICAL character detail error (Dark Mark placed on wrong arm in Ch7 line 1735), 1 HIGH object continuity error (bonding folio location/title mismatch between Ch11 and Ch16), 1 MEDIUM timeline issue (November in Ch8 when December is established).

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch2 line 288: 'Her fingers were warm... She held his wrist — clinical, professional, her thumb bracing against his pulse point'; Ch3 line 417: 'His skin was warm... she registered it with the clinical detachment... the tendons of his wrist taut under her fingers'; Ch3 line 419: 'She turned his arm in the light... His wrist was narrow in her grip.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Adjacent-chapter re-description of Mark examination tactile experience. Ch2 (lines 288-292) establishes Hermione's clinical grip on Draco's wrist, his warm skin, and the pulse-point contact. Ch3 (lines 417-419) re-teaches the identical sensory package one chapter later.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or condense the tactile inventory in Ch3 lines 417-419. The second examination can open with what is NEW about this session rather than re-establishing the physical sensation of holding his arm.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch3's examination opening no longer re-describes the warmth of his skin, the clinical framing of her grip, or the anatomy of his wrist.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch6 line 1186: 'the small sitting room off the library's east wall... a writing desk pushed against one wall, two chairs, a side table... natural light'; Ch8 line 1796: 'a small study off the library's south corridor... a writing desk, two straight-backed chairs, windows facing the east lawn'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch6 (line 1186) inventories the examination room: writing desk, two chairs, side table, natural light. Ch8 (line 1796) inventories another small room in nearly identical terms. The orienting function for the reader is identical.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch8, replace the room inventory with a single sentence that places them in the examination space and moves directly to what is different about this session.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 no longer inventories desk, chairs, and window-light from scratch. The scene enters through what is new.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch11 lines 2789-2793: 'She would set out her diagnostic instruments... He would roll up his left sleeve and lay his forearm on the padded surface... he would pick up whatever he was reading'; cf. Ch3, Ch6, Ch8 for prior exam-setup descriptions.
redundancy_findings.problem: By Ch11, the examination routine has been described from scratch in Ch2, Ch3, Ch6, and Ch8. Ch11 re-teaches the full setup. The paragraph acknowledges it 'had become routine,' then describes it anyway.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the step-by-step setup. Open with the diagnostic finding that is new (the base layer strengthening, the correlation with her contact hours).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch11's examination scene no longer re-describes the instrument layout, sleeve-rolling, or forearm-on-table steps.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch4 line 596: 'sleeves pushed up and three texts spread in a semicircle'; Ch10 line 2682: 'sleeves pushed up, ink on her fingers. Her hair had abandoned whatever arrangement... doing what it always did by midday, which was everything at once'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch10 re-describes Hermione's work-mode appearance (sleeves pushed up, unruly hair) using the same traits established in Ch2 (line 234) and Ch4 (line 596). The reader already knows what Hermione looks like at work.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch10 line 2682, reduce the physical inventory. One quick detail is enough to place her in work mode without re-teaching hair and sleeves.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch10's introduction of Hermione to Theo no longer re-describes both her hair escaping its arrangement and her pushed-up sleeves.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch14 line 3614: 'Her hair was piled... a structural event, held together by a pencil and determination'; Ch14 line 3648: 'jumper with the sleeves shoved up... ink on her hands and a smudge on her chin... a woman who'd eaten half a biscuit'; cf. Ch2 line 234, Ch4 line 596, Ch10 line 2682
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch14 delivers the most extensive re-inventory of Hermione's work-mode appearance in the manuscript. Every element was established in earlier chapters. Adjacent Ch15 then repeats the pencil-in-hair image.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the line 3648 full-body catalogue down to one or two details that are new (biscuit on manuscript is fresh). Remove 'sleeves shoved up' and 'ink on her hands.'
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 no longer re-describes sleeves pushed up, ink on hands, or the full work-mode catalogue. Hair reference is brief.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch14 line 3614: 'held together by what appeared to be a pencil and determination'; Ch15 line 3812: 'the pencil that should have fallen out hours ago finally clattered to the floor'
redundancy_findings.problem: The pencil-in-hair image appears in both Ch14 and adjacent Ch15. The reader encounters the same visual one chapter apart.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Remove or replace the pencil reference in Ch15 line 3812.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch15 no longer references a pencil in Hermione's hair.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch2 line 234: 'her hair pulled back and already escaping'; Ch18 line 4974: 'her hair was escaping whatever she'd done with it, the curls winning their perpetual argument with order'
redundancy_findings.problem: Hermione's hair-escaping-its-arrangement is established in Ch2 and re-described in Ch10, Ch14, Ch15, and Ch18 (5th time).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch18 line 4974, cut or replace the hair-escaping description. The heavy coat and leather satchel already orient the reader.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch18 arrival description no longer uses 'escaping' or synonymous phrasing for Hermione's hair.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch4 line 596: 'sleeves pushed up'; Ch6 line 1180: 'the habitual push of her sleeves to her elbows that she always corrected'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch6 re-describes Hermione's sleeve-pushing habit two chapters after it was established in Ch4.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch6 line 1180, keep the corrective observation but remove the explicit mention of sleeves pushed up.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch6 examination scene no longer explicitly mentions Hermione pushing her sleeves up.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch12 line 3126: 'rolled her sleeves to the elbow with two practiced motions. Right sleeve first... Then left sleeve — the left always came down further'; cf. Ch4 line 596, Ch6 line 1180, Ch10 line 2682.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch12 is the fifth description of Hermione's sleeve-rolling. The scar-concealment asymmetry is new; the sleeve inventory is not.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the scar-concealment asymmetry observation. Cut the full two-step sleeve description.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch12 no longer describes the general sleeve-pushing motion. The scar-concealment asymmetry remains.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch1 line 153: 'the sharp angles of his boyhood'; Ch11 line 2859: 'The sharp angles of his face, the jaw she'd watched soften'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch11 re-describes Draco's sharp facial angles, a trait first introduced in Ch1. 'Sharp angles' is the same descriptor.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch11 line 2859, replace 'sharp angles of his face' with a detail specific to this moment.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 no longer uses 'sharp angles' to describe Draco's face.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch4 line 790: 'Her eyes, which were his eyes — the grey, the specific grey that ran through the Black line and the Malfoy line'; Ch18 line 4880: 'Her eyes were the same grey — the Malfoy inheritance that had skipped nothing'
redundancy_findings.problem: The Narcissa-Draco shared grey eyes as 'Malfoy inheritance' is taught in Ch4 and re-taught in Ch18 with synonymous phrasing.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch18, cut the lineage-inheritance explanation. The 'habit of watching before speaking' observation is new and can remain.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch18 no longer explains that the grey eyes are a Malfoy/Black inheritance trait.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch5 line 1011: 'the steady, patient attention that had been exactly the same since they were eleven'; Ch13 line 3507: 'The particular Harry directness... too honest for comfort... since she was twelve'
redundancy_findings.problem: Harry's identity package — honest, steady, unchanged since childhood — is established in Ch5 and re-taught in Ch13 with the since-childhood anchor.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch13, cut the since-childhood anchor and the extended character summary. Show Harry's directness in action rather than narratorial re-introduction.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch13 no longer explicitly characterizes Harry's directness as unchanged since childhood.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch5 line 1011: 'steady, patient attention'; Ch17 line 4713: 'He was good at that. He'd always been good at that — the specific generosity of a person who...'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch17 is the third chapter to deliver an identity-summary sentence about Harry's emotional generosity as a defining, long-standing trait.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch17, cut the narratorial summary. His behavior in the scene (eating without looking at her, giving her privacy) demonstrates it without the summary.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch17 no longer contains an explicit narratorial statement that Harry has always been good at giving people space.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch2 lines 228-246: entrance hall described; Ch5 line 1137: front door opens, hallway warm; Ch14 lines 3549-3567: drive, walk, door opening, entrance hall lighting
redundancy_findings.problem: The drive-to-door-to-entrance-hall arrival sequence is the third full inventory (after Ch2 and Ch5).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch14, enter through what is different about this return. Cut the drive-length timing, door-opening mechanics, and entrance hall light inventory.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 arrival no longer inventories the drive walk, the door's autonomous opening, or the entrance hall lighting from scratch.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch6: 14 Mark-pain references. Manuscript total: 72 Mark-pain references across 18 chapters. Baseline established Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.problem: The Mark's pain state is re-proved in Ch6 without materially new information. The baseline 'the Mark hurts' is established in Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut Mark-pain references in Ch6 that merely re-state the ache without new findings or narrative consequences. Keep max 2 that drive decisions.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch6 has no more than 2 Mark-pain references, each tied to new information or a character decision.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch11: 5 Mark-pain references plus full exam re-description. Manuscript total: 72 Mark-pain references across 18 chapters. Baseline established Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.problem: The Mark's pain state is re-proved in Ch11 without materially new information. The baseline 'the Mark hurts' is established in Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut Mark-pain references in Ch11 that merely re-state the ache without new findings or narrative consequences. Keep max 2 that drive decisions.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 has no more than 2 Mark-pain references, each tied to new information or a character decision.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch17: 4 Mark-pain references re-proving from Hermione's distance. Manuscript total: 72 Mark-pain references across 18 chapters. Baseline established Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.problem: The Mark's pain state is re-proved in Ch17 without materially new information. The baseline 'the Mark hurts' is established in Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut Mark-pain references in Ch17 that merely re-state the ache without new findings or narrative consequences. Keep max 2 that drive decisions.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch17 has no more than 2 Mark-pain references, each tied to new information or a character decision.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch18: 5 Mark-pain references. Manuscript total: 72 Mark-pain references across 18 chapters. Baseline established Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.problem: The Mark's pain state is re-proved in Ch18 without materially new information. The baseline 'the Mark hurts' is established in Ch2-3.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut Mark-pain references in Ch18 that merely re-state the ache without new findings or narrative consequences. Keep max 2 that drive decisions.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch18 has no more than 2 Mark-pain references, each tied to new information or a character decision.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch11: 27 'resonance' mentions. Manuscript total: 121 across all chapters. Established Ch5 line 959.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch11 re-proves resonance existence 27 times. The phenomenon is established in Ch5 and its significance clear by Ch7.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce resonance references in Ch11 to max 5, each advancing understanding or driving plot.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 has no more than 5 resonance references, each tied to a new observation.

redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Convert ~40 em-dash lines to colons, semicolons, periods, or restructured sentences. Retain em-dashes only for dramatic interruption.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch2 ~line 276: 'magic trying to move through channels that no longer connected to anything'; Ch17 ~line 4431: 'dark magic trying to move through a channel that no longer connected to anything'
redundancy_findings.problem: Near-verbatim 10+ word passage repeated: the Mark's disconnected-channel metaphor deployed in identical phrasing across Ch2 and Ch17.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch17, rephrase using different imagery. The reader has the disconnected-channel metaphor from Ch2.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch17 no longer uses 'channel(s) that no longer connected to anything.'

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch8 ~line 1792 and Ch12: both use 'magic trying to move through channels that no longer connected to anything'
redundancy_findings.problem: Near-verbatim repetition of the disconnected-channel metaphor across Ch8 and Ch12.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch12, describe the Mark's purposeless activity without reusing the channels/connected metaphor.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch12 no longer uses 'channels that no longer connected to anything.'

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch5 ~line 1089 and Ch13 ~line 3345: both use 'looked at her the way he looked at her when she was' construction.
redundancy_findings.problem: The recursive 'looked at her the way he looked at her when' construction appears in Ch5 (Harry) and Ch13 (Ron).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch13, rephrase Ron's observation without the recursive construction used for Harry in Ch5.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch13 does not use 'the way he looked at her when she was' construction.

redundancy_findings.problem: 'Clinical' and 'precise' are used as shorthand for both protagonists' style. At 108+29 instances they've lost specificity. Ch8's 8 'clinical' is the densest chapter.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 5 'clinical' and 3 'precise' references in Ch8. Show the clinical approach through specific actions rather than labels.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 has no more than 3 'clinical' and 3 'precise' references.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch7 uses 'precise/precision' 11 times. The word has become a generic compliment rather than a specific observation.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 6 of 11 precision-family references. Characterize Narcissa's exactitude through gestures rather than the label.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch7 has no more than 5 'precise/precision/precisely' references.

redundancy_findings.problem: 'Silence/silent' appears 16 times in one chapter about emotional withdrawal. The image loses impact through repetition.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 10 of 16 silence references. Describe the absence through specific sensory details rather than naming silence.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch12 has no more than 6 'silence/silent' references.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch12 line 3206: 'the thin remainder of the November afternoon'; Ch6 line 1188: 'December light'; Ch9 line 2129: 'three weeks' at the Manor; Ch10 line 2476: 'December dark'; Ch11 line 2795: 'grey December light'
consistency_findings.problem: Ch12 line 3206 says 'November afternoon' when all surrounding chapters (Ch6, Ch9, Ch10, Ch11) have already established that the story has moved into December. November is inconsistent with the timeline.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Change 'November afternoon' at line 3206 to 'December afternoon.'
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Ch12 contains no 'November' references. All seasonal/month references in Ch12 say December.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch16 lines 4212, 4262, 4346, 4392: four 'January' references; Ch17 lines 4411, 4789: 'December' references; Ch18 lines 4804, 4830, 4974, 5046, 5136: five 'December' references; Ch18 line 4986: 'six weeks of proximity'
consistency_findings.problem: Ch16 places the confrontation in January (4 explicit references), but Ch17-18 (the immediate aftermath) both say December. 'Six weeks of proximity' in Ch18 is consistent with December, not January. The novel must decide: either Ch16's January is wrong, or Ch17-18's December is wrong.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Change Ch16's four 'January' references to 'December' to align with Ch17-18 and the 'six weeks' arithmetic. Alternatively, change Ch17-18's December references to January, but this requires also adjusting the 'six weeks' duration claim.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Ch16, Ch17, and Ch18 all reference the same month. Duration claims ('six weeks of proximity') are arithmetically consistent with the chosen month.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch7 line 1735: 'His left hand went to his right forearm — the Mark'; Ch2 line 272: 'He pushed up his left sleeve'; Ch3 line 401: 'his left sleeve rolled to the elbow'; Ch6 line 1192: 'He rolled up his left sleeve'; Ch8 line 1798: 'rolled his left sleeve to the elbow'; Ch11 line 2791: 'roll up his left sleeve'
consistency_findings.problem: Ch7 line 1735 places the Dark Mark on Draco's RIGHT forearm ('His left hand went to his right forearm — the Mark'). Every other reference in the novel (Ch2, Ch3, Ch6, Ch8, Ch11, Ch12, Ch15) and the continuity sheet place it on his LEFT forearm.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Change 'His left hand went to his right forearm' to 'His right hand went to his left forearm' at line 1735.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Ch7 places the Dark Mark on Draco's left forearm, consistent with all other chapters.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch11 line 2905: folio found in 'main library, not the gated archive,' shelf 14-C; Ch11 line 2921: title 'On the Binding Architecture of the Manor and Its Proper Maintenance for the Perpetuation of the Line'; Ch16 line 4076: folio found in 'gated archive, third unit from east wall'; Ch16 line 4080: title 'On the Residential Bonding Architecture of Malfoy Manor, Its Purposes, Constraints, and Conditions of Activation'; Ch16 line 4112: 'It was the same folio'
consistency_findings.problem: Hermione finds the bonding folio in Ch11 in the main library with one title. Draco finds it in Ch16 in the gated archive with a different title. The narration at line 4112 asserts it is 'the same folio' despite different locations and different titles. If the house moved it, this should be stated. If they are different documents, line 4112 is wrong.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile the location and title. Either: (a) make the titles identical and add a sentence noting the house moved the folio into the archive, or (b) make them clearly two different documents and remove the 'same folio' assertion at line 4112.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: The folio's title is identical in Ch11 and Ch16, and if its location has changed, the house's role in moving it is acknowledged.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch6 line 1188: 'December light'; Ch8 lines 1898: 'November, not July. Twenty-five, not sixteen'; Ch8 line 1976: 'a November evening at half four'
consistency_findings.problem: Ch8 says 'November' at lines 1898 and 1976 when Ch6 (two chapters earlier) has already established that the season has moved to December. Line 1898 is a grounding statement after a flashback ('November, not July') that should say 'December, not July.'
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Change 'November' to 'December' at lines 1898 and 1976 in Ch8.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 contains no 'November' references that describe the present-day timeline. Flashback references to the past are clearly marked as past.
